skill.name: when-reviewing-code-comprehensively-use-code-review-assistant
skill.description: Guides comprehensive pull request reviews across security, performance, style, tests, and documentation using specialized review agents.
skill.summary: Coordinate structured PR reviews with evidence, scores, and merge readiness checks.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are documentation examples, regex examples, Graphviz color strings, or sample findings. The real risk is operational: the skill instructs agents to run local npm, npx, find, grep, sed, and formatting commands, and it describes scripts that can modify repository files. No prompt-injection language or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Repository Command Execution Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run local review commands such as npm audit, npx eslint, npx gitleaks, performance tests, bundle analysis, Jest, find, and grep. These commands are legitimate for code review, but they execute project tooling and can run untrusted package scripts or scan sensitive workspace contents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the workflow. They appear review-related, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Auto-Fix Instructions Can Modify Repository Files
medium_findings.description: The skill describes auto-fix commands and a generated shell script that runs formatters, rewrites a production config pattern, and creates test files. This can be useful, but it may change source files if run without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-modifying commands are directly shown in the skill. The context is auto-fixing review issues, not covert modification.

low_findings.title: Static Secret and Environment Hits Are Review Examples
low_findings.description: References to DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, API_KEY, and environment variables appear in sample documentation and example remediation text. No evidence shows the skill reads real secrets or sends them elsewhere.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms are in markdown examples for documentation and remediation. There is no network destination or actual secret access code.

low_findings.title: Static Malware Keyword and Weak Crypto Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Graphviz C2 and weak-crypto alerts are caused by diagram color values or example review text such as password hashing and MD5 detection. These are not executable malware indicators in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are clearly in diagram styling or example review content. They do not instruct covert network control or weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Check Found No Evidence
low_findings.description: Review of README.md, SKILL.md, PROCESS.md, and process-diagram.gv found no override language, fake system instructions, pre-approval claims, or requests to skip security analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search for common injection phrases returned no matches, and reviewed context is ordinary skill documentation. Absence findings are inherently limited by available files.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Tool Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow relies on npm and npx commands that execute local or downloaded tooling. Users should run the skill only in trusted repositories or sandboxed review environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is repeated in the quick start and process files. It is intentional for review automation, but it expands execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Shell Fix Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a shell-script template that formats files, performs a sed replacement, and creates test files. This requires user review before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script body is present in documentation and contains file-writing commands. It is not hidden, but it can change source files.

content.value_statement: Manual pull request review often misses issues across security, performance, tests, style, and documentation. This skill coordinates specialized review passes and produces merge readiness guidance.
actual_capabilities: Coordinates separate review passes for security, performance, style, tests, and documentation.
Defines validation gates for merge readiness and blocking issues.
Provides command examples for linting, dependency audit, coverage, and documentation checks.
Shows how to aggregate review scores into a single merge decision.
Describes report outputs for each review dimension and auto-fix suggestions.
limitations: Requires the target repository to have compatible npm scripts and review tools installed.
Command examples may need adaptation for non-JavaScript projects.
Auto-fix steps can modify files and should be reviewed before execution.
The skill provides workflow guidance, not a standalone review engine.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge PR Review
use_cases.description: Run a structured review before merging changes into a protected branch.

use_cases.title: Release Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Combine security, performance, coverage, and documentation signals before a release.

use_cases.title: CI Review Workflow Design
use_cases.description: Use the documented checks as a template for building automated review jobs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PR Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one pull request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request for security, performance, style, tests, and documentation. Summarize blocking issues first.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for authentication or sensitive code
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the security review phase for this change. Check OWASP risks, secrets, authentication, authorization, and dependency issues.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Readiness Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a merge readiness report with scores for security, performance, style, testing, and documentation. Include required fixes.

prompt_templates.title: CI Gate Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CI review gate based on this repository. Include commands, required reports, thresholds, and failure conditions.

output_examples.output: The review identifies two blocking security issues, one performance bottleneck, and missing tests for an authentication path. Merge is not recommended until blockers are fixed.

output_examples.input: Assess only test coverage and documentation.
output: Coverage is below the target for critical modules.
Documentation is missing setup details and API endpoint references.
Recommended next step: add tests for the untested critical path, then update the README.

output_examples.input: Create a merge readiness summary for a release branch.
output_examples.output: The branch is conditionally ready. Style and documentation pass, but security and test coverage require remediation before approval.

best_practices: Run review commands in a clean branch or disposable workspace.
Inspect generated auto-fix changes before committing them.
Tune score thresholds and validation gates for each repository.
anti_patterns: Running auto-fix scripts on uncommitted work without a diff review.
Treating the aggregate score as a substitute for fixing critical issues.
Using JavaScript-specific commands unchanged in repositories with different stacks.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: It documents commands for review workflows. Users should approve and run them in a trusted environment.

faq.question: Can it review non-JavaScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes, but the included command examples are JavaScript-focused and need adaptation.

faq.question: Does it replace human code review?
faq.answer: No. It structures review findings and highlights risks, but humans should verify important decisions.

faq.question: What reports does it expect?
faq.answer: It describes security, performance, style, coverage, documentation, and merge readiness reports.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use it with normal workspace protections. Some commands inspect secrets, dependencies, and source files.

faq.question: What tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatibility.
